# B001. Bayesian Linear Return Forecaster

## Positioning

A calibration-first return forecaster that prefers a stable posterior over a complex state machine. It should produce a confidence-aware alpha score that can be blended with the repo's existing regime and risk gates.

## Reference Basis

- [Bayesian Online Changepoint Detection](../../references/papers/0710.3742_bayesian_online_changepoint_detection.pdf) - changepoint prior and shrinkage guard
- [Multivariate Bayesian Structural Time Series Model](../../references/papers/1801.03222_multivariate_bsts.pdf) - structural trend and seasonal baseline

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### Market Data and Features

Point-in-time data loading plus the shared feature builder used by most signal stacks.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - point-in-time market data loading
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - technical, lagged, and derived feature construction

### Regime and State

Regime detection and state overlays used when the signal should shrink or route rather than trade blind.
- [src/models/hmm.py](../../../src/models/hmm.py) - latent regime detector

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Use point-in-time returns, OHLCV, and the technical feature bundle from `src/features/builder.py`.
- Feed latent regime state from `src/models/hmm.py` as a shrinkage input rather than as a hard trading rule.
- Keep the loader aligned to the same freshness rules enforced by `src/risk/manager.py` and `src/monitoring/health.py`.

## Execution Path

- Estimate a posterior mean and interval for next-period return.
- Convert posterior confidence into a long/flat or top-k allocation score.
- Send the score through the same backtest and paper-safe gates used by the current executable baseline.

## Risk Controls

- Reject stale data and low-liquidity windows before the posterior is used.
- Do not trade when posterior variance is wide enough to invert the expected edge.
- Keep turnover inside the configured cost budget; otherwise the signal stays in research mode.

## Validation Gates

- Compare calibration, hit rate, and risk-adjusted return against the current LightGBM baseline.
- Check whether the posterior remains stable under regime shifts and during drawdown clusters.
- Re-run the existing smoke and backtest path on the same assets used for F001 so the comparison is apples-to-apples.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive it if the posterior cannot beat the feature baseline after fees.
- Archive it if calibration depends on leaked or late-arriving inputs.
- Archive it if the model only works with aggressive parameter tuning rather than a stable prior.

## Next Step

Use this as a calibration benchmark for B007 and B018 rather than as a standalone production candidate.
